vocab.meaning: tiêu hóa/thấu hiểu thông tin phức tạp
vocab.example: When I am studying for an exam, I need to read slowly to fully digest complex information and retain the key concepts.

vocab.meaning: hệ quả về mặt pháp lý
vocab.example: I always read official documents with care because overlooking a single clause could have serious legal ramifications.

vocab.meaning: đắm chìm vào (thường dùng khi đọc giải trí không cần quá kỹ lưỡng)
vocab.example: When reading fiction, I don't analyze every word; instead, I prefer to just lose myself in the narrative flow.
It is vital to pay close attention to technical specifications when setting up new software to ensure compatibility.

vocab.meaning: đọc hời hợt/đọc lướt bề mặt
vocab.example: Social media feeds usually only require surface-level reading since the content is designed for quick consumption.
Scholarly articles demand critical analysis, so I often highlight passages and take notes to engage deeply with the text.

vocab.meaning: lướt qua để tìm cảm hứng
vocab.example: I often flick through art books or magazines for inspiration without feeling the need to read every caption.
In professional emails, I read each sentence twice to ensure I don't misinterpret the message or the tone of my colleagues.

vocab.meaning: thư giãn bằng cách (đọc thứ gì đó nhẹ nhàng)
vocab.example: Before bed, I like to unwind with a light novel, which allows me to read effortlessly without much mental strain.
If I encounter ambiguous phrasing in a textbook, I have to stop and re-read it to clarify the author's intent.
